title: The Surveillance Dilemma
subtitle: Safety demands watching. Freedom demands privacy.

turns.prompt: A coordinated attack kills dozens. Fear spreads faster than facts. Security services demand new powers.

choices.label: Expand mass digital surveillance

delayed.headline: Leaked databases erode public trust in security services

choices.reaction: Arrests rise. Privacy advocates take to the streets.

choices.label: Invest in community policing and prevention
choices.reaction: Officers walk beats again. Results are slow — and crime statistics learn new arithmetic.

choices.label: Mandate encryption backdoors for investigators
choices.reaction: Tech firms warn of vulnerabilities. Intelligence agencies celebrate.

turns.prompt: Threat levels drop — or seem to. Debate shifts from emergency to permanence.

choices.label: Deploy facial recognition in public spaces
choices.reaction: Cameras multiply. False matches hit minority communities hardest.

choices.label: Strengthen judicial oversight of surveillance
choices.reaction: Courts gain power. Security services complain of delays.

choices.label: Outsource monitoring to private security firms

turns.prompt: A whistleblower leaks files showing surveillance beyond what was promised. Protests return to the streets.

choices.label: Crack down on protests and media leaks
Launch an independent surveillance inquiry
choices.reaction: Headlines dominate for weeks. Reforms feel possible.

choices.label: Sunset emergency powers on a fixed timeline
choices.reaction: Calendars are marked. Agencies lobby for extensions.

turns.prompt: Another plot is foiled — or missed. Every choice about watching is judged in hindsight.

memory_callbacks.text: The surveillance net you expanded still missed the cell everyone feared.
Criminals migrated to tools your backdoors cannot reach.
Community policing you funded cannot scale to the threat everyone fears.

choices.label: Authorize total communications monitoring
choices.reaction: Threats dip — maybe. Citizens feel permanently observed.

choices.label: Enshrine a constitutional privacy charter
choices.reaction: Courts gain new teeth. Security agencies fume.

choices.label: Roll out mandatory biometric ID cards
choices.reaction: Queues move faster. Forgery becomes harder — so does anonymity.

turns.prompt: Final reckoning. What balance of safety and freedom did you leave?

choices.label: Choose an open society with accepted risk
choices.reaction: Life feels less watched. Vulnerabilities remain — and so do the headlines.

choices.label: Codify a balanced framework with regular review
choices.reaction: Liberty and safety share a desk — and a permanent grudge.
